extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIČ attacked the Monte Lussari mountain TT, gained 40 seconds on THOMAS despite a mid-climb mechanical, overturning a 26-second deficit to win the Giro by 14 seconds.
tactical_patterns: ROGLIČ started faster on flat TT prologue despite trailing GC, banking time before climbing gradients favored him—reverse-pacing strategy worked.
THOMAS changed helmet and bike at exchange, losing precious seconds in transition while ROGLIČ minimized stop time—exchange execution matters.
ROGLIČ overcame mid-climb mechanical without total collapse, suggesting superior form reserves allowed recovery unlike his 2020 Tour TT disaster.
MCNULTY posted top-3 intermediate time, revealing INEOS underestimated Jumbo-Visma's climbing TT depth when handicapping stage.

rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: gained 40s despite mechanical on 14% gradients, showing TT climbing form peak exactly when needed
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: lost 40s to ROGLIČ on mountain TT after holding 26s lead, suggesting insufficient steep-gradient power
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: neutral: held third at 1:15 down, becoming first Portuguese grand tour podium since 1970s but no GC threat
rider_signals.MCNULTY Brandon: positive: posted top-3 split time as domestique, indicating exceptional climbing TT form unused for team tactics
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: went 2 seconds faster than MCNULTY in earlier wave, foreshadowing Jumbo-Visma's superior mountain TT preparation
rider_signals.VINE Jay: neutral: led briefly as Australian TT champion before being overtaken, showing early starter disadvantage
rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: negative: led intermediate split then suffered mechanical, collapsing from potential stage win to outside top-5
